feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business Technology Platform
feed.subtitle: SAP Business Technology Platform Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Optimizing SAP On-Premises to Azure SQL Server Data Pipeline for Scalability
entry.content:
I have configured a data pipeline to facilitate real-time or near-real-time data transfer between an SAP on-premises server and Azure SQL Server.

Current Setup:
Established a VPN connection to securely access the SAP on-premises database, enabling authentication-based access.
Implemented an ELT pipeline to load data into Azure SQL Server efficiently.
Additionally, configured an OData connection to extract and transfer data.
Currently, the setup functions well. However, I am concerned about its scalability as data traffic increases in the future. To ensure seamless performance, I am looking for ways to optimize the existing process.

I would appreciate insights, recommendations, and discussions on improving this setup for better efficiency and handling higher data volumes. Open to exploring alternative connectivity and data transfer approaches.

Looking forward to expert suggestions from the SAP Community!

Regards;
Prashant Marathe

entry.title: 403 forbidden error for XSUAA protected capm destination, the role restricted entity.
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I want to restrict an enitity in cds with READ only for a specific role, but getting 403 error.
My cap application is xsuaa enabled and configured as OAuth2UserTokenExchange destination in cf and my ui5 app is consuming that destination. and All the roles and role collection configured correctly

Below are configurations I made in my app
package.json

xs-security.json

{
  "xsappname": "capm_backend",
  "tenant-mode": "dedicated",
  "scopes": [
    {
      "name": "$XSAPPNAME.Viewer",
      "description": "Viewer role"
    }
  ],
  "attributes": [],
  "role-templates": [
    {
      "name": "VIEWER_ROLE",
      "description": "Viewer role",
      "scope-references": [
        "$XSAPPNAME.Viewer"
      ],
      "attribute-references": []
    }
  ],
  "authorities": [
    "$ACCEPT_GRANTED_AUTHORITIES"
  ],
  "oauth2-configuration": {
    "token-validity": 43200,
    "redirect-uris": [
      "https://applicationstudio.cloud.sap",
      "https://*.hana.ondemand.com/login/callback",
      "https://*.cfapps.us10.hana.ondemand.com/login/callback"
    ],
    "refresh-token-validity": 3600
  },
  "xsenableasyncservice": "true"
}

service.cds

service BookServices @(requires: 'authenticated-user') {

    entity BookData @(restrict: [{
        grant: ['READ'],
        to   : 'VIEWER_ROLE'
    }])                                      as projection on book.BookData;
}

I am trying to access the BookData end point from my ui5 app it's returning 403 error

entry.title: S/4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD Monitor Email Transmissions
entry.content:
Hello,

I am currently working on sending emails in the demo development system via ABAP development. Even if I send mail successfully with 'Sent' status, I cannot view its attachments and body text in "Monitor Email Transmissions" Application just as in the attached screenshot.

Is there any other way to ensure I send mail with correct body and attachments?

entry.title: Unable to subsribe integration suite getting error subscription failed with error code 400
Call Action Project error queryA ENOTFOUND in Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Destination configuration 

we configured the S4HANA On-premise URL as a Destination like in a below screenshot.

Action Project

Process Automation Flow

We added the above Action project as a Dependency to our process automation flow like in a below screenshot.

Problem we are facing

Below is the response of the action activity 'GET_MAIL_LIST'.

I saw the similar issue in the below community link : Call web service error queryA ENOTFOUND - SAP Community However it didn't help us to resolve the issue.

Kindly help me to figure out the issue.

SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP Build Process Automation 

Thanks in advance.

Best Regards,

Sakthi.

entry.title: Race Condition Issue in SAP CAP (Node.js) on BTP Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
Scenario:

We have a portal where suppliers can create collections. Each supplier logs in with their credentials, and their user session is used to track and process their actions.

  1. Supplier B starts creating a collection by sending a request to our CAP service.
  2. At the same time, Supplier A is making multiple API requests (e.g., fetching data, updating records, etc.).
  3. When Supplier B completes the creation process, instead of their own user email being assigned, the system mistakenly assigns Supplier A's email to the newly created collection.

This unexpected behavior suggests a race condition where user identity (possibly stored in req.user) is being overwritten or incorrectly managed during concurrent requests.

Question:

  • How can we ensure that the correct user identity is maintained throughout each request lifecycle in SAP CAP?
  • Could this be related to how session handling or request context is managed in CAP?

Environment:

  • SAP CAP: @SAP/cds: ^7
  • Database Adapter: @SAP/cds-hana: ^2
  • Authentication: @SAP/xssec: ^3, passport: ^0.7.0
  • HTTP Client: @SAP-cloud-sdk/http-client: ^3.20.0
  • Deployment: SAP BTP Cloud Foundry

Has anyone encountered a similar issue? Any suggestions or insights would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: SAP BTP free tier account
entry.content:
Hi ,

Can some one please help how to get SAP BTP free tier account. I am looking out for it in order to go for SAP GenAI certification.

Thanks 

Anita Yadav

entry.title: Does BTP Cloud Foundry have the concept of service accounts or service principals?
entry.content:
We have multiple CAP Applications that we deploy using our CI/CD (Azure Pipelines).

The problem right now is that the account being used is tied to a user which is a no-no. It should be a service account or service principal.

I am having trouble finding resources on how to create an service account that isn't tied to a user. Anything I do find requires that the account to be created have an email address. I tried just creating a user via the CF Cli and it doesn't allow me to.

Is this even supported by BTP Cloud Foundry?

entry.title: Re: display fiori elements draft edit status in listreport
entry.content:
I found that the Common.SemanticKey annotation solved this issue

annotate myService.myEntity with @Common: {SemanticKey: [title]};

entry.title: Could Race Conditions Occur in This SAP CAP Scenario?
entry.content:
I'm investigating a potential race condition in an SAP CAP service where req.user or cds.context might be overwritten by another request during an internal POST call.

Scenario:

  • The TesteC event posts data to the TesteA entity using a service connection to the same CAP application (cds.connect.to('TesteRace')).
  • The before CREATE handlers for TesteA and TesteB log and modify the req.user information before the record is created.
  • When TesteC is triggered multiple times concurrently (also posting to TesteB), could req.user or cds.context.user be overwritten during the before CREATE phase?

Here is a simplified version of the code:

const cds = require('@sap/cds');
const LOG = cds.log();

module.exports = class TesteRace extends cds.ApplicationService {
  init() {
    this.on('TesteC', async (req) => {
      try {
        const srv = await cds.connect.to('TesteRace');

        LOG.info(`Executing TesteC - User from req.user: ${req.user?.attr?.email || req?.user?.id || 'empty'}`);
        LOG.info(`Executing TesteC - User from cds.context: ${cds.context.user.attr.email || cds.context?.user?.id || 'empty'}`);

        return await srv.post(this.entities.TesteA).entries([{ nome: req?.data?.nome || Math.random() * 1000 }]);
      } catch (error) {
        req.error(error);
      }
    });

    this.before('CREATE', this.entities.TesteA, async (req) => {
      req.data.nome = `${req.user?.attr?.email || cds.context?.user?.id || cds.context?.user?.attr?.email} ${Math.random() * 1000}`;
      LOG.info(`Before CREATE TesteA - User from req.user: ${req.user.attr.email || 'empty'}`);
      LOG.info(`Before CREATE TesteA - User from cds.context: ${cds.context?.user?.id || cds.context?.user?.attr?.email || 'empty'}`);
    });

    this.before('CREATE', this.entities.TesteB, async (req) => {
      req.data.nome = `${req.user?.attr?.email || cds.context?.user?.id || cds.context?.user?.attr?.email} ${Math.random() * 1000}`;
      LOG.info(`Before CREATE TesteB - User from req.user: ${req.user.attr.email || 'empty'}`);
      LOG.info(`Before CREATE TesteB - User from cds.context: ${cds.context?.user?.id || cds.context?.user?.attr?.email || 'empty'}`);
    });

    return super.init();
  }
}

Issue:

  • In the SAP BTP trial environment, I could not reproduce a race condition.
  • However, in the customer’s environment, req.user seems to be overwritten when the internal POST call occurs.
  • If I remove the internal POST call, the issue disappears.
  • The scenario is hard to reproduce, even in the customer’s environment—out of 4,000 records created, only 17 were affected by this condition.
  • The email from one request is being overwritten by another request from a different user, which suggests that cds.context might not be properly isolated between requests.

Has anyone experienced a similar issue? Could cds.context unexpectedly be shared between requests? Any insights would be greatly appreciated! 🚀

entry.title: How to fetch known error in Action call for SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hi experts,

we have created a process within SAP Build Process automation. The process includes an action call after an approval task.

Within the CAP application which is handling the action call we have defined known errors that will be returned as follows:

return req.error({
    code: 123, // internal code for identifying return messages
    message: "my return message",
    target: "where is the message caused",
    status: 4## //the error status
})

An error could be that a specific action was not done before approval by the approver himself.

However, if the action does not retrieve a success return (HTTP return status equals 4##) the whole process is put to an error state. Is there any possibility to catch this error within the process itself and send a notification to the MyInbox of a specific group/user?

Actually, we don't want the process to get into an error state for this scenario but instead execute an alternative path within the process itself.

 

regards

Emanuel

entry.title: What is the quickest way to transfer data from an on-premise SAP ECC system to SAP BTP?
entry.content: we have a few applications built in ABAP RAP running on SAP BTP environment. These apps need to access some real time data on our SAP ECC on-premise system. I want to understand what is the best and the fastest way which brings good performance. Should we use OData to fetch the data or should we use RFC to fetch the data. Based on my search on the internet it seems that RFC is much faster than OData. Has anyone tried both of them and has found one better over the other?

entry.title: Not able to assign policy to API proxy
entry.content:
Dear All,

I am following the below blog but not able to assign policy as per step 12-13 in S4 Hana trial CPI and API management tool.

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-starter-isuite-api-management..html

I have applied the cloud platform connectivity policy on API proxy . At policy editor my Preflow count is showing only 0 unlike value 7 as show in technical blog. Hence not able to edit any steps inside the policy .

Did I miss anything ?

entry.title: Document Information Extraction - XML File Support - Error E30
entry.content:
Hello everyone, 

I am trying to process XML Invoices via Document Information Extraction. According to the documentation, filetype XML is supposed to be supported for invoices. When trying to upload XML Files via the Web-UI, an Error-Message is shown, describing "Error: Files of this filetype can not be processed (Code: E30).

After further inspecting the network traffic, HTTP RC 415 - Unsupported Media Type is returned for Content type text/xml.
Can somebody assist here? Already tried multiple different Instances & XML Files. 

Thanks!

entry.title: manifest file has two different application configured but only one tile appears in Build Workzone
entry.content:
Hi All,

Background: I have one sap ui5 application which launches two different application based on custom parameter.

Requirement : I want this application to be deployed in SAP Build Workzone as two separate applications.
There are two crossNavigation added to the manifest.json file of the project. 

Issue: However only one project shows up in SAP Build Workzone. Can someone suggest how to fix this.

"crossNavigation": {
"inbounds": {
"atom-ui-claims-inbound": {
"signature": {
"parameters": {
"appmode":{
"defaultValue": {
"value" : "claims"
}
}
},
"additionalParameters": "allowed"
},
"semanticObject": "Claims",
"action": "manage",
"title": "Claim Management",
"subTitle": "3.0",
"icon": "sap-icon://request"
},
"atom-ui-claimfinance-inbound": {
"signature": {
"parameters": { },
"additionalParameters": "allowed"
},
"semanticObject": "ClaimsBulk",
"action": "manage",
"title": "Claim Processing",
"subTitle": "Finance",
"icon": "sap-icon://my-sales-order"
}
}
}

entry.title: Cannot connect to a service - Build code to SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hello, I am developing an extension in SAP Build Code, and when trying to add a SSFF service, it does not appear as available.

The BTP Trial account has already been registered in the SSFF product, and the destination has been created.

 

As you mension in the help is not available for Full-Stack Application Using Productivity Tools dev space.

 

I wonder to know if there is another way to add a SSFF Service in Sap build code in a Full-Stack Application project using Productivity Tools dev space.

Has anyone else experienced the same issue? Thanks in advance!

entry.title: HANA upgrade
entry.content:
Hello Team,

As part of SAP's continuous maintenance cycles and in accordance with their notification, we upgrade the SAP HANA Cloud Database to the latest available version. 

There are two subaccounts in which we are trying to add the application "SAP HANA CLOUD" under subscription, but there is no "tools" plan.

Could you please guide us on it.

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Custom Approuter - Frontend Backend Communication
entry.content:
How to Restrict Backend Access to Frontend Only in SAP BTP (SAP UI5 + AppRouter + XSUAA)?

Hey everyone,

I’m currently working on an SAP UI5 application that is deployed via a Custom AppRouter. This app communicates with a backend service that extracts a specific user attribute (myspecialnumber) from the JWT token(we need that myspecialnumber to be displayed int the frontend). The authentication is handled via SAP XSUAA, and the users log in using our corporate Entra ID (Azure AD).

So far, everything works as expected:

  • The frontend fetches user data by calling https://mybackend.cfapps.eu11.hana.ondemand.com/backend/userinfo.

  • The backend is secured using JWT authentication and verifies the token before returning the requested data.

Issue: Unrestricted Backend Access via Browser

Currently, any logged-in user can directly access the backend API by simply entering the URL in the browser:

👉 https://mybackend.cfapps.eu11.hana.ondemand.com/backend/userinfo

This means that users can bypass the frontend and call the backend API directly. However, I want to ensure that only my UI5 application can communicate with the backend, and anyone trying to access it manually (e.g., via the browser or Postman) should get a 403 Forbidden error.

How Can I Secure This?

How should we modify our architecture or security setup to achieve this? Any best practices or recommendations would be highly appreciated!

Below are my current configurations:

📌 xs-app.json (AppRouter Configuration)

{
  "welcomeFile": "/index.html",
  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/backend/(.*)$",
      "target": "/$1",
      "destination": "my-backend",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa"
    },
    {
      "source": "^/(.*)$",
      "target": "$1",
      "localDir": "./webapp",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa"
    }
  ]
}

📌 manifest.yaml (Deployment Configuration)

applications:
  - name: my-approuter
    memory: 128M
    buildpacks:
      - nodejs_buildpack
    command: npm start
    routes:
      - route: myapprouter.cfapps.eu11.hana.ondemand.com
    services:
      - xsuaa-myapprouter
    env:
      destinations: >
        [
          {
            "name": "my-backend",
            "url": "https://mybackend.cfapps.eu11.hana.ondemand.com",
            "forwardAuthToken": true
          }
        ]

📌 Node.js Backend Route (API Endpoint for User Info)

app.get('/userinfo', passport.authenticate('JWT', { session: false }), (req, res) => {
  console.log('GET /userinfo request received');
  const authHeader = req.headers.authorization;
  console.log('Authorization Header:', authHeader);

  if (!authHeader || !authHeader.startsWith('Bearer ')) {
    console.log('Invalid Authorization Header');
    return res.status(403).send('Invalid authorization header.');
  }

  const token = authHeader.split(' ')[1];
  console.log('Token:', token);

  xssec.createSecurityContext(token, uaaService, (error, securityContext) => {
    if (error) {
      console.error('Error processing token:', error.message);
      return res.status(403).send(`Token processing error: ${error.message}`);
    }

    const myspecialnumber = securityContext.getAttribute("myspecialnumber");  
    const userInfo = {
      email: securityContext.getEmail(),
      name: securityContext.getLogonName(),
      given_name: securityContext.getGivenName(),
      family_name: securityContext.getFamilyName(),
      myspecialnumber: myspecialnumber,
      token: token
    };

    console.log('User Info:', userInfo);
    res.json(userInfo);
  });
});

📌 UI5 Frontend Fetch Request

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <title>MYApp</title>
  <script id="sap-ui-bootstrap" src="https://ui5.sap.com/resources/sap-ui-core.js"
    data-sap-ui-theme="sap_fiori_3"
    data-sap-ui-libs="sap.m"
    data-sap-ui-compatVersion="edge"
    data-sap-ui-async="true"
    data-sap-ui-resourceroots='{
      "myapp": "./"
    }'>
  </script>
  <script>
    function fetchUserInfo() {
      console.log('Fetching user info...');
      
      fetch('backend/userinfo', { method: 'GET' })
      .then(response => {
        console.log('Response received:', response);
        if (!response.ok) {
          throw new Error('Network response was not ok');
        }
        return response.json();
      })
      .then(data => {
        console.log('User info received:', data);
        document.getElementById('userInfo').innerText = `User: ${data.name} (${data.email})`;
      })
      .catch(error => {
        console.error('Error fetching user info:', error);
      });
    }

    sap.ui.getCore().attachInit(function () {
      console.log('SAP UI5 Core initialized');
      sap.ui.require(["sap/ui/core/ComponentContainer"], function (ComponentContainer) {
        console.log('Creating ComponentContainer...');
        new ComponentContainer({
          name: "myapp",
          height: "100%",
        }).placeAt("content");
        console.log('ComponentContainer placed at content');
      });
      
      fetchUserInfo();
    });
  </script>
</head>
<body class="sapUiBody">
  <div id="userInfo" style="padding: 10px; font-weight: bold;">Loading user information...</div>
  <div id="content"></div>
</body>
</html>

entry.title: Add Transactional Behavior to Your Core Data Services + debug Behavior implementation
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

i followed this tutorial and succeed finish the job. 

Add Transactional Behavior to Your Core Data Services

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-environment-transactional-enablement.html

But i got a problem, i want to debug the Behavior implementation,after i set the external break-point, it was not triggerred.

i was using a trial subaccount.

Could you please guide me how to debug it, thanks !!!!

entry.title: How to connect and test CAP application locally with destinations using hybrid profile?
entry.content:
I do not want to deploy and test application, as developer, I would like to test app locally before push.
Running hybrid profile worked but returned empty array.

Why it shows empty array to me? What did I miss?

It works in Postman.
@showkath_naseem @LucasBorin @massimiliano_cardosi 

cds watch --profile hybrid
cds serve all --with-mocks --in-memory? --profile hybrid 
live reload enabled for browsers
resolving cloud service bindings...
bound destinations to Cloud Foundry managed service opps-mta-destination:opps--destination-key
bound auth to Cloud Foundry managed service opps-mta-xsuaa:opps-mta-xsuaa-key
----
[cds] - mocking OPPS_DataAccessV4 { path: '/odata/v4/opps-data-access-v4' }
[cds] - server listening on { url: 'http://localhost:4004' }
----

I referred these posts: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/btp-cap-how-to-connect-to-remote-services-locally-in-cap-application/ba-p/13550070

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-cap-hybrid-testing/ba-p/13556582

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/btp-cap-connect-to-remote-service-locally-hybrid/qaq-p/12662393
